title: cerastium triviale var. viscosum
intro: Cerastium triviale var. viscosum, also known as Sticky Mouse-ear or Sticky Chickweed, is a perennial herbaceous plant of the family Caryophyllaceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which can help soothe skin irritations, wounds, and minor infections. The plant has been used to support overall health, with potential benefits including reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and boosted immune function. Cerastium triviale var. viscosum can be prepared as a tincture, salve, or infused oil, making it a versatile herbal remedy for various health issues.
